title: 10 best herbal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs, which are used as an alternative or complementary treatment for this condition. Peripheral arterial occlusive disease is a circulatory disorder caused by narrowing or blockage of the arteries in the limbs, often resulting in pain, numbness, and non-healing wounds. Herbal tinctures, such as ginkgo biloba, turmeric, and gotu kola, can help improve peripheral arterial occlusive disease by promoting blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and strengthening blood vessels. These tinctures can significantly improve patients' lives by reducing symptoms, increasing mobility, and preventing complications like tissue damage and amputation. For instance, a patient with peripheral arterial occlusive disease who regularly takes ginkgo biloba tincture may experience improved blood flow to their legs, leading to reduced pain and an increased ability to walk and perform daily activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains a compound called aescin, which has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. This can help alleviate symptoms of the disease, such as cramping, pain, and numbness in the legs, by improving the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the affected tissues. Additionally, horse chestnut tincture may help to reduce the swelling and fluid buildup that can occur in the legs and feet due to poor circulation. By promoting overall vascular health, horse chestnut tincture can help manage the symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease and improve quality of life for those affected.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horse chestnut tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, improving blood flow and alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease. This is due to the presence of Aescin, a saponin compound.

Venotonic: Horse chestnut tincture's venotonic property helps strengthen the walls of veins and improve their elasticity, promoting better blood circulation in the limbs, which is crucial for peripheral arterial occlusive disease. Aescin is also responsible for this property.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of horse chestnut tincture helps protect blood vessels from oxidative damage, preserving their function and integrity, which is essential for managing peripheral arterial occlusive disease. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, such as quercetin, in the tincture.

Anti-edematous: Horse chestnut tincture's anti-edematous property helps reduce the accumulation of fluid in the tissues, alleviating swelling and discomfort in the limbs, a common symptom of peripheral arterial occlusive disease. Aescin is the primary active constituent responsible for this property.

Improves microcirculation: Horse chestnut tincture's ability to improve microcirculation helps ensure adequate blood supply to the smallest blood vessels, promoting healing and tissue regeneration in the limbs affected by peripheral arterial occlusive disease. Aescin and other active compounds in the tincture contribute to this property.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the horse chestnut plant contain escin, a saponin that has been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, making it useful for peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Bark: The bark of the horse chestnut plant contains aescin and other triterpene saponins, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and vasoprotective effects, making it useful for treating chronic venous insufficiency and peripheral arterial disease.

Flowers: The flowers of the horse chestnut plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for improving circulation and reducing inflammation in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried aesculus hippocastanum leaves and flowers in late summer and fall when mature.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 3-4 days.
Grind 100g of dried horse chestnut material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 50g of the powdered horse chestnut with 250ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and steep at room temperature for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids before transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles and storing at cool temperatures.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains active compounds that can help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. These compounds, such as allicin, have been shown to help relax blood vessels, reduce plaque buildup in arteries, and improve circulation. As a result, garlic tincture can help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, such as cramping, pain, and numbness in the legs. Additionally, garlic has been found to have anticoagulant properties, which can help prevent blood clots from forming and further obstructing blood flow. Overall, garlic tincture can be a valuable addition to a treatment plan for peripheral arterial occlusive disease, helping to improve symptoms and promote overall vascular health.
remedy_properties: Antiplatelet aggregation: Garlic tincture inhibits the clumping of platelets in the blood, reducing the risk of blood clots and improving blood flow in peripheral arteries. This property is due to the active constituents ajoene and allicin.

Fibrinolytic activity: Garlic tincture has the ability to break down blood clots and improve blood flow, which is beneficial for peripheral arterial occlusive disease. The active compound responsible for this property is allicin.
Vasodilation: Garlic tincture helps widen blood vessels, increasing blood flow and oxygen supply to affected limbs. The active constituent responsible for this property is allicin.

Antioxidant properties: Garlic tincture protects blood vessels from oxidative damage, promoting overall vascular health. The active compounds responsible for this property include allicin, diallyl disulfide, and diallyl trisulfide.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Garlic tincture reduces inflammation in blood vessels, which can help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease. The active constituents responsible for this property include allicin, ajoene, and diallyl sulfides.
remedy_parts: Bulbs: Garlic bulbs are widely used to make medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to their high concentration of allicin, a compound known for its cardiovascular benefits.
Stems: Although not as potent as bulbs, garlic stems can also be used in tinctures for their mild cardiovascular properties and as a more sustainable alternative.
Cloves: Packed with active compounds, garlic cloves are commonly used in tinctures for their ability to improve blood flow and reduce the risk of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh garlic bulbs and wash them thoroughly under cool running water.
Chop the cleaned garlic into small pieces and macerate in vodka or everclear spirit at ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing flavors to mature and mingle.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids.
Store the finished herbal garlic tincture in a dark glass bottle, labeling with date and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it has anti-inflammatory and circulation-boosting properties. This condition is characterized by the narrowing or blockage of arteries in the limbs, often leading to pain, cramping, and poor wound healing. Arnica's anti-inflammatory effects can help alleviate symptoms by reducing inflammation in affected areas. Moreover, its ability to improve circulation can help increase blood flow to the limbs, promoting healing and reducing the risk of tissue damage. By addressing these key aspects of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, arnica flower tincture can provide relief and support overall vascular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and helenalin, help reduce inflammation in peripheral arterial occlusive disease, improving blood flow and alleviating pain.

Circulatory stimulant: Arnica flower tincture stimulates blood circulation, thanks to compounds like thymol and flavonoids, which can help improve peripheral arterial occlusive disease by reducing the risk of blood clots and promoting healing.

Analgesic: Arnica flower tincture has analgesic properties, attributed to compounds like helenalin and flavonoids, which help alleviate pain and discomfort in peripheral arterial occlusive disease by reducing inflammation and swelling.

Anticoagulant: Arnica flower tincture has anticoagulant properties due to compounds like coumarins and salicylic acid, which can help prevent blood clots and improve blood flow in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Tissue repair: Arnica flower tincture promotes tissue repair, thanks to compounds like flavonoids and tannins, which can help heal damaged tissues and promote recovery in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The arnica flowers are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, making them ideal for treating peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Rhizomes: Arnica rhizomes contain high levels of thymol, which has been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, making them a valuable component in medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Buds: Arnica buds are a potent source of helenalin, a compound with powerful anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in the treatment of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Leaves: Arnica leaves contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a useful addition to medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers or purchase high-quality dried flowers.
Dry the flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air drying.
Combine one part of the dried flowers with five parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days shaking daily to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Belladonna tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains powerful alkaloids that can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation. The active compounds in belladonna, such as atropine, scopolamine, and hyoscyamine, can help relax smooth muscle in blood vessel walls, which can increase blood flow and reduce the symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease. By promoting vasodilation and improving circulation, belladonna tincture may help alleviate pain, cramping, and other symptoms associated with this condition. However, it is important to note that belladonna should only be used under the guidance of a healthcare provider, as it can have potent effects and interactions with other medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-spasmodic: Belladonna tincture helps relieve muscle spasms in peripheral arteries, improving blood flow, due to the presence of alkaloids like hyoscyamine and scopolamine.
Vasodilation: It dilates blood vessels, increasing oxygen and nutrient supply to affected areas, by containing the anticholinergic alkaloid, hyoscyamine.
Analgesic: Belladonna tincture alleviates pain in peripheral arterial occlusive disease by blocking pain signals with its alkaloids, such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory effects, attributed to alkaloids like hyoscyamine, reduce inflammation in blood vessels, promoting better blood flow.
Slows heart rate: Belladonna tincture helps regulate heart rate, allowing more time for blood to circulate, thanks to its anticholinergic properties, primarily hyoscyamine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain alkaloids such as atropine and scopolamine, which can help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected areas.
Flowers: Contain essential oils and flavonoids, which can help to improve blood flow and reduce the symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Seeds: Contain high levels of fatty acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve overall cardiovascular health.
Bark: Contains alkaloids and tannins, which can help to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh atropa bella donna leaves and flowers, ensuring a clean and dry workspace.
Chop 20-30 grams of plant material into small pieces, releasing their medicinal properties.
Combine chopped plant material with 40% ethanol or vodka in a glass jar, filling to the top.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking daily, allowing for efficient extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids, and transfer to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian frankincense tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing pain in the legs. Additionally, boswellic acids have been shown to inhibit the enzyme that damages the lining of blood vessels, which can help prevent the progression of the disease. Furthermore, the tincture can help improve overall vascular health by reducing oxidative stress and improving the elasticity of blood vessels. Therefore, Indian frankincense tincture can be a valuable addition to a treatment plan for peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Indian frankincense tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituents like boswellic acid, helps reduce inflammation in peripheral arteries, improving blood flow and alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Improved microcirculation: The ability of Indian frankincense tincture to enhance microcirculation, attributed to its active compounds such as olibanum, aids in delivering oxygen and nutrients to affected tissues, promoting healing and tissue regeneration in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Analgesic: The analgesic property of Indian frankincense tincture, resulting from its active constituents like incensole acetate, helps alleviate pain caused by peripheral arterial occlusive disease, enhancing patients' comfort and mobility.

Anticoagulant: Indian frankincense tincture's anticoagulant property, due to its active compounds like boswellic acid, helps prevent blood clots and promotes blood flow in peripheral arteries, reducing the risk of complications in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Fibrinolytic: The fibrinolytic activity of Indian frankincense tincture, attributed to its active constituents like incensole acetate, helps break down blood clots and improves overall blood flow in the peripheral arteries, aiding in the management of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the Indian frankincense plant contains boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help improve blood flow in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Seeds: The seeds of the Indian frankincense plant contain essential oils and fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Gum resin: The gum resin exuded by the Indian frankincense plant contains boswellic acids and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Buds: The buds of the Indian frankincense plant contain high concentrations of boswellic acids and other active compounds, making them a potent source for medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality boswellia serrata resinous gum from sustainably sourced trees weighing 500 grams.
Grind the harvested resin into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine 200 grams of the powdered resin with 400 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar within 15 minutes.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily, to allow maceration and solvent extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids, then bottle and label the tincture for storage within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the arteries, which can help alleviate the symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease. Additionally, white tea has been shown to help reduce the buildup of plaque in the arteries, which can further improve blood flow and reduce the risk of complications associated with the disease. The herbal properties of white tea may also help to relax and dilate blood vessels, which can improve overall circulation and reduce the risk of damage to the extremities.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, improving blood flow and alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, such as catechins and epicatechins.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in white tea tincture helps protect blood vessels from oxidative stress and damage, promoting better circulation and preventing further progression of peripheral arterial occlusive disease. The primary antioxidants responsible are polyphenols, including epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG).

Anticoagulant: White tea tincture's anticoagulant property helps prevent blood clots from forming, reducing the risk of thrombosis and embolism in peripheral arterial occlusive disease. This is attributed to the presence of coumarins and flavonoids.

Vasodilatory: White tea tincture's vasodilatory effect helps widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of ischemia in peripheral arterial occlusive disease. This is due to the presence of theaflavins and thearubigins.

Antihyperlipidemic: White tea tincture's antihyperlipidemic property helps lower levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and triglycerides, reducing the risk of atherosclerosis and plaque buildup in peripheral arterial occlusive disease. This is attributed to the presence of catechins, theaflavins, and thearubigins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, white tea leaves can help improve endothelial function and reduce inflammation in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Buds: High in flavonoids, white tea buds can help improve blood flow and reduce the risk of blood clots in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Bark: Containing active compounds that can help dilate blood vessels, white tea bark can improve circulation and reduce symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh camellia sinensis leaves and flowers for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dark place for 24-48 hours to prevent oxidation.
Use a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to grind the dried herb into a fine powder.
Mix 1 part ground herbal white tea with 5 parts high-proof vodka or organic ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the tincture mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily, before straining and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains compounds that can improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. These compounds, such as asiaticoside and madecassoside, have been shown to strengthen the walls of blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce the buildup of plaque in the arteries. By promoting the growth of new blood vessels and improving the overall health of the circulatory system, gotu kola tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, such as cramping, numbness, and pain in the legs. Additionally, gotu kola has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can further support the health of the cardiovascular system and reduce the risk of complications associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Gotu kola tincture helps to widen blood vessels, improving blood flow in peripheral arteries, which is important for peripheral arterial occlusive disease. The active constituents responsible for this property are saponins, such as asiaticoside and madecassoside.

Improved microcirculation: Gotu kola tincture aids in the circulation of blood in the smallest blood vessels (capillaries), which is crucial for delivering oxygen and nutrients to affected limbs in peripheral arterial occlusive disease. The triterpene compounds, including brahmoside and brahminoside, contribute to this property.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Gotu kola tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for peripheral arterial occlusive disease as it can help alleviate symptoms like pain and swelling in the affected limbs. The triterpene acids, such as asiatic, madecassic, and maslinic acids, are responsible for these anti-inflammatory effects.

Improved wound healing: Gotu kola tincture promotes wound healing, which is crucial for peripheral arterial occlusive disease as it can help prevent and treat ulcers and sores that may develop due to poor circulation. The active constituents responsible for this property are triterpenes, including asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Antioxidant properties: Gotu kola tincture has antioxidant effects, which are important for peripheral arterial occlusive disease as they can help protect blood vessels from oxidative damage. The phenolic acids, such as caffeic, chlorogenic, and p-coumaric acids, contribute to these antioxidant effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation.

Stems: Gotu kola stems contain similar medicinal properties to the leaves, and are often used in tinctures to help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, such as pain, numbness, and cramping in the legs.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of gotu kola contain high levels of asiaticoside, madecassoside, and other triterpene compounds, which have been shown to improve circulation and reduce swelling, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Roots: Although not as commonly used as the leaves, stems, and rhizomes, the roots of gotu kola do contain some medicinal properties and may be included in tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease to help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried centella asiatica leaves measure out 50 grams of material.
Grind the herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle method takes approximately.
Combine the ground herb with 250 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar mix thoroughly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for extraction steep for at least.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another container discard the solids and label the tincture preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains active compounds that can improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. These compounds include cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to increase blood vessel dilation and improve circulation. Additionally, cinnamon has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, preventing the buildup of plaque and clots that can lead to peripheral arterial occlusive disease. By improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, cinnamon tincture can help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease, such as cramping, pain, and numbness in the legs.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cinnamon tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, improving blood flow and alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease. This is mainly due to the presence of cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in cinnamon tincture helps protect the blood vessels from oxidative stress, reducing the risk of plaque buildup and promoting overall vascular health. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are polyphenols, such as cinnamtannin B1.

Blood sugar regulation: Cinnamon tincture can help regulate blood sugar levels by increasing insulin sensitivity and reducing blood glucose levels. This is crucial for peripheral arterial occlusive disease as high blood sugar levels can contribute to vascular damage and impaired wound healing. The active compounds responsible for this property are methylhydroxychalcone polymer and Type-A polymers.

Antiplatelet aggregation: Cinnamon tincture inhibits platelet aggregation, preventing the formation of blood clots that can further obstruct blood flow in peripheral arteries. This property is attributed to cinnamaldehyde and epicatechin.

Vasodilation: Cinnamon tincture has vasodilatory effects, causing blood vessels to widen and improve blood flow to the limbs. This is particularly beneficial for peripheral arterial occlusive disease as it helps alleviate symptoms such as cramping, numbness, and pain. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are cinnamaldehyde and procyanidins.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the cinnamon tree is commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to its potential ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation.

Seeds: Cinnamon seeds are used in tinctures for their potential to improve blood circulation and reduce the risk of blood clots, which can help alleviate symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Leaves: Cinnamon leaves are often included in tinctures because of their potential to dilate blood vessels, which can help improve circulation and reduce the symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees weighing at least 2-3 pounds.
Clean the harvested bark by rinsing with distilled water and scrubbing off dirt particles gently.
Dry the cleaned bark in a warm dark place for 2-4 weeks or using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Combine one part dried cinnamomum verum bark with two parts vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar at room temperature.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily to extract the bioactive compounds and then strain through cheesecloth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound. Curcumin helps improve endothelial function, which is often impaired in individuals with peripheral arterial disease. It does this by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, improving blood flow, and preventing the formation of plaque in the arteries. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to improve symptoms of intermittent claudication, a common symptom of peripheral arterial disease, by increasing walking distance and reducing pain. Overall, turmeric tincture can help manage peripheral arterial occlusive disease by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and preventing the progression of the disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its curcumin content, helps reduce inflammation in peripheral arterial occlusive disease, improving blood flow.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of turmeric tincture, provided by curcumin, combats oxidative stress, protecting blood vessels and promoting vascular health in the disease.

Antiplatelet: Turmeric tincture's ability to prevent platelet aggregation, thanks to curcumin, reduces the risk of blood clots and improves overall circulation in peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
Fibrinolytic: Turmeric tincture's fibrinolytic activity, attributed to curcumin, helps dissolve blood clots and aids in maintaining proper blood flow in the affected areas.

Vasodilatory: The vasodilatory effect of turmeric tincture, resulting from curcumin, helps widen blood vessels and improve blood circulation, alleviating symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem (rhizome) is used to make medicinal tinctures due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help improve blood flow and reduce symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant, closely related to the rhizomes, contain curcuminoids that may help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them useful for treating peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Leaves: Turmeric leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their potential wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties, which could contribute to improving symptoms of peripheral arterial occlusive disease.

Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain curcuminoids, which have been studied for their potential to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them a potential component in medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried turmeric root curcuma longa and organic vodka at a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Combine the turmeric root with organic vodka in a clean glass jar for 6-8 weeks of maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding solids after 24 hours.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature away from light for 2 years.
Shake the tincture daily during its 6-month maturation period before using as a dietary supplement.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tincture helps with peripheral arterial occlusive disease because it contains active compounds that can improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. Antioxidant: The tincture has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are often associated with peripheral arterial occlusive disease. The antioxidant effects are primarily due to the presence of flavonoids.

Antiplatelet: Maidenhair tree tincture can inhibit platelet aggregation, preventing the formation of blood clots that can further obstruct blood flow in peripheral arteries. This is attributed to the presence of triterpenes in the tincture.

remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the maidenhair tree is used to make medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to its anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which can help improve blood flow in the affected areas.

Leaves: The leaves of the maidenhair tree are used in medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation.

Stems: The stems of the maidenhair tree are used in medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease due to their ability to improve blood flow and reduce the risk of blood clots, which can help alleviate symptoms of the disease.

Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree are used in medicinal tinctures for peripheral arterial occlusive disease because they contain compounds that can help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote the growth of new blood vessels.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 part ginkgo biloba powder with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, shaking daily for 14 days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, discarding solids, and store in a cool dark place.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat peripheral arterial occlusive disease with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of hawthorn, ginkgo biloba, and prickly ash. Hawthorn improves blood flow and strengthens blood vessels, ginkgo biloba increases circulation and reduces inflammation, while prickly ash stimulates blood flow and alleviates pain. Consistently taking these tinctures, as directed, can help manage symptoms and promote overall vascular health. Always consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: It is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment, including herbal tinctures.
